display.purpose: Hero and decisive release statements only.

shadows.name: patch-offset
shadows.purpose: Separates the generated patch from the signal illustration.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Initial section reveal, removed under reduced-motion preferences.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The direct, release-state action used once per content moment.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary">Windows release status <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 13 13 3M6 3h7v7" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg></button>

components.name: Signal Patch
components.description: A compact generated-patch object that proves editable output without becoming a dashboard.

narrative.overview: A light, near-monochrome brand surface built around proof, not spectacle. It uses real product evidence, exact typography, and choreographed but optional motion to make the audio-to-patch workflow legible.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper and instrument ink, never pure black and white.
Crafted display type with a precise interface sans.
Flat structure and fine rules rather than decorative panels.
Release state is always honest and explicit.

rules.name: Evidence Rule
rules.body: Signal Green clarifies a transformation or action and never competes with product evidence.

rules.name: Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Elevation appears only when an object must separate from an illustration or respond to interaction.

dos: Do use real app screenshots, spectrograms, and approved audio examples.
Do preserve reduced-motion alternatives for every choreographed reveal.
donts: Don't use neon, cyberpunk, sci-fi control-room, or glowing-grid aesthetics.
Don't use generic AI SaaS cards, oversized metrics, or empty claims.
Don't add complicated decorative elements, dense dashboards, glassmorphism, or gradient text.
